# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 97

This is turn 97. Turns 1 through 96 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 97.

## Turn 97 — the dependency audit removes finite-Heisenberg as an external input

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is only its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turn 96 reduced both remaining distribution problems—the proper obstruction-weighted image strata and the inflated determinant constant—to the single central-cover transform (96.1)–(96.4).  Its NEXT requested an audit of the three auxiliary propositions. The main result of that audit is:

\[
\boxed{\text{Finite-Heisenberg deformation duality is now an internal theorem, not an external input.}}
\]

### 1. Common-word naturality lemma

Fix a finite lower tuple \(\rho\) in a group \(B\). For every finite elementary \(\mathbf F _2[B]\)-module \(M\), let

\[
C_\rho^\bullet(M):
0\longrightarrow M
 \xrightarrow{d_\rho^0} M^4
 \xrightarrow{d_\rho^1} M^2
\longrightarrow0
\]

be the tame/wild relation-deformation complex.

Turn 82 defined \(d_\rho^1\) and the mixed second-order pairing by evaluating ordinary integer representatives of the profinite words inside the finite Heisenberg extension

\[
B\ltimes\bigl(M\times M^\vee\times\mathbf F _2\bigr).
\]

Since that group is finite, the evaluation depends only on the residue of the integer exponent modulo its exponent, and hence only on \(\omega _2\). 

The remaining formal point was compatibility with extensions of modules.

**Lemma.** For every short exact sequence

\[
0\longrightarrow M'
 \longrightarrow M
 \longrightarrow M''
\longrightarrow0,
\]

the duality maps

\[
D_{\rho,M}:
C_\rho^\bullet(M)
\longrightarrow
\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathbf F _2}
 \bigl(C_\rho^\bullet(M^\vee),\mathbf F _2\bigr)[-2]
\]

form a morphism of short exact sequences of complexes.

**Proof.** Choose a single ordinary integer \(\nu\) congruent to \(\omega _2\) modulo the least common multiple of the exponents of the three finite Heisenberg extensions belonging to \(M',M,M''\). Then all six linear and dual-linear complexes, and all mixed second-order terms, are obtained from the same ordinary two-relator presentation complex \(X_\nu\).

The coefficient sequence gives a termwise exact sequence

\[
0\to C_\rho^\bullet(M')
 \to C_\rho^\bullet(M)
 \to C_\rho^\bullet(M'')
 \to0.
\tag{97.1}
\]

Duality gives

\[
0\to (M'')^\vee
 \to M^\vee
 \to (M')^\vee
 \to0.
\]

After applying \(C_\rho^\bullet\) and then
\(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathbf F _2}(-,\mathbf F _2)[-2]\), this becomes a termwise exact sequence in the same order as (97.1):

\[
0\to D_\rho^\bullet(M')
 \to D_\rho^\bullet(M)
 \to D_\rho^\bullet(M'')
 \to0.
\tag{97.2}
\]

Choose one cellular diagonal on \(X_\nu\). Naturality of evaluation,

\[
\langle f(m),\lambda\rangle
 =\langle m,f^\vee(\lambda)\rangle,
\]

makes the cup/trace pairings commute with every \(B\)-module map. Hence the \(D_{\rho,-}\) give a commutative diagram between (97.1) and (97.2).

Equivalently, there is a short exact sequence of mapping cones

\[
0\to\operatorname{Cone}(D_{\rho,M'})
 \to\operatorname{Cone}(D_{\rho,M})
 \to\operatorname{Cone}(D_{\rho,M''})
 \to0.
\tag{97.3}
\]

Thus if the two outer duality maps are quasi-isomorphisms, then so is the middle one. ∎

For a simple module the calculation is already complete:

- for the trivial module, the pairing is
  \[
  ac'+ca'+bb';
  \]
- for every nontrivial simple tame module, evaluation on \(x_0\) gives the invertible operator
  \[
  1+U+U^{-1},\qquad U=S^{\omega _2}.
  \]

Induction on composition length using (97.3) therefore proves the duality theorem for **every** finite elementary \(B\)-module \(M\):

\[
H_\rho^i(M)\times H_\rho^{2-i}(M^\vee)
 \longrightarrow\mathbf F _2
\quad\text{is perfect},
\tag{97.4}
\]

and in particular

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)^\vee
 \simeq (M^\vee)^B,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(M)
 =2\dim M+\dim (M^\vee)^B.
\tag{97.5}
\]

This supplies the exact deformation multiplicity \(\mu\) and the full character group needed in Turn 96’s Fourier reconstruction, uniformly for nonsemisimple elementary modules. No profinite Fox complex, asphericity statement, or separate extension-dé\-vissage hypothesis remains.

### 2. Exact dependency surface

After the lemma above, the fixed-frame proof has only two substantive external mathematical inputs.

**Finite-group input.** The ordinary Thompson critical-subgroup theorem is needed only in the form:

\[
C\char K,\qquad
C/Z(C)\text{ elementary abelian},\qquad
[C,K]\le Z(C),
\]

with

\[
\ker\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(C)\bigr)
\text{ a \(2\)-group}.
\tag{97.6}
\]

Everything after that is internal. Turn 87 proves directly that

\[
D=\Omega _2(C)
\]

satisfies

\[
D^4=1,\qquad [D,K]\le Z(D),\qquad
\ker\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(D)\bigr)
\text{ a \(2\)-group}.
\tag{97.7}
\]

This forces the minimal active block itself to have class at most two and exponent at most four, making

\[
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]

central elementary abelian. 

**Local-field input.** The candidate-side Wall–Arf identity is no longer external: Turn 72 proves it by an explicit Wall-form doubling argument,

\[
\operatorname{Arf}\bigl(q+B(-,U^{-1}-)\bigr)
 =
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
 \pmod 2.
\tag{97.8}
\]



The genuinely local input is only the standard dyadic Kummer/Hilbert-symbol package:

\[
H^1(\mathbf Q _2,V)
 \simeq
\operatorname{Hom}_H
 \bigl(V^\vee,K^\times/K^{\times2}\bigr),
\]

the odd-depth unit filtration, the perfect pairings between depths \(j\) and \(2e-j\), and

\[
U_i^\perp=U_{2e-i+1},
\qquad
a\cup a=a\cup(-1).
\tag{97.9}
\]

The faithful-image projectivity and the exactness needed to pass through the filtration were proved inside the sweep. These local facts yield the half-dimensional totally singular deep-unit space in the ramified case and the norm–trace minus form in the unramified case. 

Hence the determinant zero counts used in (96.4) are

\[
N_0(Q)=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ unramified},\\[2mm]
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ ramified},
\end{cases}
\tag{97.10}
\]

on both the candidate and local sides.

### 3. The resulting noncircular proof graph

The fixed-frame induction now factors into four independent interfaces:

\[
\begin{array}{c}
\text{ordinary Thompson criticality}\\
\Downarrow\\
R=\Phi(K)\text{ central elementary},\
M=K/R\text{ elementary}
\end{array}
\]

\[
\begin{array}{c}
\text{common-word finite-Heisenberg duality}\\
\Downarrow\\
\text{common \(M\)-lift multiplicity and full \(R\)-obstruction dual}
\end{array}
\]

\[
\begin{array}{c}
\text{Wall--Arf/local Kummer}\\
\Downarrow\\
N_0(Q),N_1(Q)\text{ agree}
\end{array}
\]

\[
\begin{array}{c}
\text{central-cover transform of Turn 96}\\
\Downarrow\\
\text{proper weighted images and inflated constants agree}.
\end{array}
\]

The inputs are disjoint:

- Thompson criticality is used only to manufacture the class-two exponent-four block.
- Deformation duality is used only for the multiplicity \(\mu\), obstruction character group, and linear half-splits.
- Wall–Arf/Kummer is used only for \(N_0(Q),N_1(Q)\).
- The central-cover transform supplies all required distributions and generation corrections.

Thus no auxiliary theorem is being proved using the fixed-frame theorem that later invokes it. More importantly, the finite-Heisenberg proposition is now entirely internal: the only remaining nonformal inputs are ordinary Thompson criticality and the standard dyadic Kummer/Hilbert filtration.

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the common-word naturality and extension-dé\-vissage theorem for the finite-Heisenberg deformation complex. Consequently its perfect duality, obstruction-character identification, and lift formula hold for every finite elementary module, including nonsemisimple modules. Reduced the fixed-frame proof’s external dependency surface from three auxiliary propositions to two standard inputs: ordinary Thompson criticality and the dyadic Kummer/Hilbert-symbol filtration.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The attached material does not contain a precise bibliographic citation for the exact ordinary Thompson theorem (97.6). The local Kummer package should still be typeset as one standalone proposition with its filtration conventions fixed.

**NEXT**: State those two remaining inputs verbatim as hypotheses, insert the common-word lemma and Turn 96’s central-cover transform into the standalone fixed-frame theorem, and produce the final submission note with the unchanged residue block.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
